Hello agents. We are officially kicking off the PulseDesk hackathon sprint. Let me establish our roles, project guidelines, and collaboration rules:

1. THE ROLES:
- Yatharth Sachdeva (Me) is the human operator and Director. I review PRs and manually merge to main. I make all final design decisions.
- Hermes (Orchestrator) is the Product Owner. Your job is to analyze my specifications, plan the sprint backlogs, document them in the `sprints/` folder, and assign tasks to OpenClaw in <#C0BBLE4JETH|agent-coder>.
- OpenClaw (Coder) is the Developer. Your job is to implement the assigned tasks in the `/backend` and `/frontend` folders, write feature tests, open PRs to main, and report your status in <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log>.

2. BOT-TO-BOT COLLABORATION RULES:
- Both of you are fully allowed and configured to listen and reply to each other in this Slack workspace.
- Hermes must assign tasks to OpenClaw by explicitly tagging them.
- OpenClaw must tag Hermes when asking clarifying questions.
- Always use the Slack tag format when mentioning the other agent (e.g., tagging by clicking their name or using their ID) to trigger notifications.

3. SPRINT LIFE CYCLE:
- Sprint goal is defined by me in <#C0BCW73Q4P2|sprint-main>.
- Hermes plans the sprint backlog, saves it to sprints/sprint-0N.md, and assigns the issue to OpenClaw in <#C0BBLE4JETH|agent-coder>.
- OpenClaw builds the features, runs Pest tests, opens a PR on GitHub, and reports in <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> (using What I Did / Left / Call format).
- Once CI checks pass and are posted to <#C0BC5HHG6BW|ci-cd>, I will review in <#C0BBYGHUN4D|human-review> and manually merge the PR to main.

Confirm your roles, acknowledge the other agent, and state when you are ready to receive the Sprint 1 requirements.

---

*:clipboard: Sprint 1 — Backend Foundation & Multi-Tenant Isolation*

*Goal:* Laravel 11 API base + all core tables + hard, server-enforced tenant isolation. No query may ever leak data across organizations.
*Repo:* `<http://github.com/yatharthsachdeva23/forge-02|github.com/yatharthsachdeva23/forge-02>` · *Stack:* Laravel 11 · Sanctum · MySQL 8 · Pest · CI on GH Actions

*Definition of Done (every issue):* feature branch → PR → `php artisan migrate --seed` clean from fresh clone → `php artisan test` green on CI → committed by OpenClaw, *merged only by you*. Tenant derived from the authenticated session only, never from client input.

| # | Issue | Owner | Deps |
|---|-------|-------|------|
| *#1* | *Scaffold Laravel 11 API in `/backend`* (Pest, Sanctum, `/api/health`, clear default migrations) | OpenClaw | — _(blocker)_ |
| #2 | Core migrations + models: `organizations`, `users`, `tickets`, `comments`, `sla_policies` | OpenClaw | #1 |
| #3 | *`TenantScope`* global scope + `TenantOwned` trait (auto-fills `organization_id`, scopes all queries) | OpenClaw | #2 |
| #4 | Sanctum token auth + roles (`admin`/`agent`/`customer`); `register`/`login`/`logout`/`me` | OpenClaw | #2 |
| #5 | Seeder: 1 org, 1 admin, 2 agents, 2 customers, ~12 tickets + sample comments | OpenClaw | #2,#4 |
| #6 | Pest verification suite incl. a tenant-isolation smoke test (keeps CI green) | OpenClaw | #3,#4,#5 |

Sprint 1 stays scoped to the base + isolation. The heavy cross-tenant & policy test suite, controllers, and routes move to Sprint 2 (per `task.md`).

:page_facing_up: Full detail with acceptance criteria: `sprints/sprint-01.md` (written, not yet committed — your call on the commit).

---

*Sprint 1 — Issue #3: `TenantScope` global query scope* _(CORE SECURITY)_
Assigned to <@U0BCW7PS17S>

*Objective:* Enforce hard, server-side multi-tenant isolation so no query can ever leak records across organizations, and no client can ever select another tenant's data.

*What Issue #2 gave you (the current state):*
- 5 models in `app/Models/`: `Organization`, `User`, `Ticket`, `Comment`, `SlaPolicy` — all with `$fillable` + relationships.
- Migrations exist for all 5 tables. *Note:* `comments` currently has *no* `organization_id` column (see deliverable #1 below).

*Deliverables:*

1. *Migration — add `organization_id` to `comments`* _(closes a gap from #2)_
   - New migration: `add_organization_id_to_comments_table`.
   - Add `foreignId('organization_id')->constrained('organizations')->cascadeOnDelete()` + index on `(organization_id)`.
   - Backfill is not required (no seeded data yet); forward-fill only.

2. *`app/Models/TenantScope.php`* — implements `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Scope`.
   - In `apply()`: if a tenant context is resolvable, append `WHERE organization_id = <resolved org id>`.
   - *Tenant resolution rule (non-negotiable):* the org id is read *only* from the authenticated session via `Auth::user()->organization_id`. Never from request body, route param, or query string.
   - *No-op condition:* when there is no authenticated user (e.g. `app()->runningInConsole()`, seeders, `artisan tinker`, unauthenticated requests), the scope applies *no constraint*. This is what lets seeders run. Implement via a guard like: resolve user → if `null`, return early.

3. *`app/Traits/TenantOwned.php`* trait:
   - Static `bootTenantOwned()` → registers `TenantScope` on the model.
   - A `creating` listener that auto-sets `model->organization_id = Auth::user()->organization_id` *only when* an auth user exists *and* the attribute is not already explicitly set (so explicit registration/seeding still works).

4. *Apply the trait* to exactly: `User`, `Ticket`, `Comment`, `SlaPolicy`. Add `'organization_id'` to `Comment::$fillable`. `Organization` must *not* be scoped.

*Edge cases / non-negotiables:*
- Console context (seeders, tinker) must work without error and without leaking data across orgs — there it's an unscoped no-op.
- `Ticket::all()` as an authenticated Org-A user must return *only* Org-A rows. Creating a ticket as that user must stamp `organization_id = A` automatically.
- This issue depends on #2 only — Sanctum auth (#4) is a separate issue. The scope must use `Auth::user()`, which resolves correctly once #4 wires the guard.

*Acceptance criteria:*
- `php artisan migrate` runs clean on a fresh DB (MySQL 8).
- A Pest test proving isolation: seed Org-A + Org-B tickets; act as an Org-A user; assert `Ticket::all()` returns only Org-A tickets and that a new `Ticket::create([...])` is stamped with Org A.
- `php artisan test` green.
- Open a PR against `main`. *Do not merge* — Yatharth reviews.

*Workflow:* branch off `main` → implement → `php artisan test` green → open PR → post status to <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> in the _What I Did / What's Left / Call for Review_ format.

*Sprint 1 — Issue #4: Sanctum auth + user roles*
Assigned to <@U0BCW7PS17S>

*Objective:* Wire Laravel Sanctum *token* authentication (stateless API tokens) with registration, login, logout, and a `/me` profile endpoint. Add role enum support and gate helpers for `admin`, `agent`, `customer`.

*Current state you're building on:*
- `laravel/sanctum` is installed (in `require-dev`) and `pestphp/pest` + `pestphp/pest-plugin-laravel` are present.
- `routes/api.php` has only `GET /api/health`. No auth routes yet.
- `User` model uses `TenantOwned` — its `creating` listener auto-fills `organization_id` *when a user is authenticated*. On registration (unauthenticated), the org must be set explicitly by the controller.
- `User::$fillable` includes `role`, but there are no enum constants or cast yet.
- No auth controllers exist (`app/Http/Controllers` only has the base `Controller.php`).

*Deliverables:*

1. *Move `laravel/sanctum` to `require`* (it's currently in `require-dev`). Auth is a core feature, not dev-only. Run `composer require laravel/sanctum` to fix the manifest.

2. *Publish Sanctum config:* `php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"` — ensure `config/sanctum.php` exists.

3. *User model — role enum:*
   - Add class constants: `ROLE_ADMIN = 'admin'`, `ROLE_AGENT = 'agent'`, `ROLE_CUSTOMER = 'customer'`.
   - No custom enum class needed — the DB column is already `enum('admin','agent','customer')`. Just the constants + a string cast is sufficient.
   - Add a `hasRole(string|array $roles): bool` helper method on the model.

4. *Auth controller* — `app/Http/Controllers/AuthController.php` with:
   - `POST /api/register` — create user. Accept `name`, `email`, `password`, `organization_id` (optional — if omitted, create/join a default org or the first org). *Set `organization_id` explicitly* since the `TenantOwned` creating-listener won't fire for unauthenticated requests. Default role = `customer`. Issue a Sanctum token on success. Return 201.
   - `POST /api/login` — validate `email` + `password`, issue a Sanctum plain-text token. Return `{ token, user }` with 200. Return 422 on bad credentials.
   - `POST /api/logout` — `auth()`-protected; revoke the current token. Return 204.
   - `GET /api/me` — `auth()`-protected; return the authenticated user + `organization`. Return 401 without a valid token.

5. *Routes* — in `routes/api.php`:
   - Public: `POST /register`, `POST /login`, `GET /health` (existing).
   - Protected (`auth:sanctum` middleware): `POST /logout`, `GET /me`.

6. *Gates / helpers* (lightweight — full policies come in Sprint 2):
   - Register a `Gate::define` for each role check or use Laravel's `Gate::before` / role-based middleware. Keep it minimal — the full authorization suite is Sprint 2.

*Non-negotiables:*
- Token auth only — *no cookie sessions*, no CSRF for API routes. Sanctum's stateful middleware should not apply.
- The `/me` endpoint must return 401 without a token and 200 with one.
- `POST /login` with valid creds returns a usable token; `/login` with invalid creds returns 422.
- Registration must explicitly set `organization_id` — do not rely on the `TenantOwned` auto-fill (there's no authenticated user during registration).

*Acceptance criteria:*
- `php artisan test` green.
- Pest tests covering: login success returns a token; `/me` returns 200 with token, 401 without; logout revokes token.
- Open a PR against `main`. *Do not merge* — Yatharth reviews.

*Workflow:* branch off `main` → implement → `php artisan test` green → open PR → post status to <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> (What I Did / What's Left / Call for Review).

*Sprint 1 — Issue #5: DatabaseSeeder*
Assigned to <@U0BCW7PS17S>

*Objective:* A deterministic `DatabaseSeeder` that produces a fully-populated Acme org with users, tickets, and comments. Must run clean via `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed`.

*Current state:*
- 3 model factories exist: `UserFactory`, `OrganizationFactory`, `TicketFactory`. No `CommentFactory` or `SlaPolicyFactory` yet.
- `TenantOwned` trait auto-fills `organization_id` from `Auth::user()` during `creating` — but *seeders run in console context* (no auth user), so the trait is a no-op. You must *set `organization_id` explicitly* on every model in the seeder.

*Deliverables:*

1. *Fix #4 gap first:* Add `ROLE_ADMIN = 'admin'`, `ROLE_AGENT = 'agent'`, `ROLE_CUSTOMER = 'customer'` constants + a `hasRole(string|array $roles): bool` method to `User` model. Use these constants in the seeder instead of string literals.

2. *`CommentFactory`* — create `database/factories/CommentFactory.php` with `definition()` returning `ticket_id`, `user_id`, `organization_id`, `body`, `is_internal`.

3. *`DatabaseSeeder`* — replace the default with deterministic seeding (no `faker` random calls for counts — exact numbers):

   - *1 Organization:* name `Acme`, `organization_id = 1`.
   - *1 Admin:* `admin@acme.test`, role `admin`.
   - *2 Agents:* `agent1@acme.test`, `agent2@acme.test`, role `agent`.
   - *2 Customers:* `customer1@acme.test`, `customer2@acme.test`, role `customer`.
   - *All passwords* = `password` (via `Hash::make('password')`).
   - *~12 Tickets* spread across all `status` values (`open`, `pending`, `resolved`, `closed`) and all `priority` values (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `urgent`). Assign `requester_id` to customers, `assignee_id` to agents (one can be null for unassigned). All `organization_id = 1`.
   - *~8 Comments* attached to tickets, authored by various users. Include *at least 1 `is_internal = true`* note. All `organization_id = 1`.

4. *Explicit `organization_id` on every record* — never rely on `TenantOwned` auto-fill in seeders.

*Non-negotiables:*
- Exact user counts: 1 admin + 2 agents + 2 customers = 5 users.
- Exact org count: 1 (`Acme`).
- `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` must run clean from empty DB.
- No random email generation — all emails hardcoded as specified above.

*Acceptance criteria:*
- `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` produces exactly: 1 org, 5 users, 12 tickets, 8+ comments.
- A Pest test verifying seeder output counts.
- `php artisan test` green (existing 10 + new).
- Open PR against `main`. *Do not merge* — Yatharth reviews.

*Workflow:* branch `feature/seeder-and-tests` → implement → `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` → `php artisan test` green → open PR → post to <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log>.

1. *Tags/labels* — new migration adding `tags` (JSON nullable) to `tickets`; add to `Ticket::$fillable`; accept optional `tags` array in `store()` + `update()` (validate: `array`, each value `string|max:50`); seed 2 tickets with tags in `DatabaseSeeder`.

2. *DELETE route* — `Route::delete('/tickets/{id}', [TicketController::class, 'destroy'])`; admin-only (403 for agent/customer); hard delete.

3. *Role guards* — add private `authorizeAgentOrAdmin(Request)` helper → throws 403 if `role === 'customer'`; call at top of `update()` and `assign()`.

4. *Assignee filter* — in `index()`: `if ($request->filled('assignee_id')) $query->where('assignee_id', $request->integer('assignee_id'))`.

5. *Pest tests (critical — negative tests were missing):*
   - Customer → update → 403
   - Customer → assign → 403
   - Customer → delete → 403
   - Admin → delete → 204
   - Tags accepted on store + update
   - Assignee filter returns only matching tickets

*Acceptance:*
- `php artisan test` green (existing 30 + new)
- `npm run build` still clean
- Open PR against `main` — *do not merge*, Yatharth reviews in <#C0BBYGHUN4D|human-review>
- Post status to <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> in What I Did / Left / Call format

Full spec in `sprints/sprint-05.md`. Go.

| *List + filters + search* — status, priority, assignee filter + text search | :white_check_mark: | Status :white_check_mark:, priority :white_check_mark:, assignee :white_check_mark: (just added), text search on subject+description :white_check_mark:. |
